Text: Complexity is the dominant factor Low complexity 2001 2004 about 1 point 5 hours no additional document requests Medium complexity 2002 about 26 hours 1 additional document request High complexity 2003 2005 about 48 to 77 hours 2 to 3 additional document requests Clear correlation higher complexity more document requests longer durations

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Region analysis Region A 2001 Low 1 point 5h and 2003 High 48 point 3h Region B 2002 Medium 25 point 9h 2004 Low 1 point 4h 2005 High 77 point 1h Region B high complexity versus Region A high complexity Region B high complexity takes significantly longer But low complexity is fast in both regions So region alone is not the cause but there may be an interaction Region B high complexity claims take longer than Region A

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Resource analysis Adjuster Mike handled 2001 fast low and 2003 slow high Adjuster Lisa handled 2002 medium slowish 2004 fast low 2005 very slow high So adjuster speed seems driven by complexity not the person both adjusters handle low complexity quickly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: However looking at the document request loops Lisas case 2005 had 3 rounds with big gaps Mikes case 2003 rounds were shorter So Lisas document collection rounds seem slower though this could be customer response time

Text: The dominant delay is the Request Additional Documents loop each round adds roughly a day waiting for customer documents presumably Case 2005 3 rounds 3 days Case 2003 2 rounds 2 days Case 2002 1 round 1 day

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the root cause chain High complexity multiple additional document requests each request round introduces about 20 to 30h waiting likely customer response time extended lead time

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Region B plus high complexity is worst suggesting either Adjuster Lisa document handling in Region B is slower or Region B customers are slower to respond or there is a staffing issue

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Also Manager Bill handles only high complexity approvals and takes about 19 to 23h for approval while Manager Ann straight through approvals are minutes If Manager Bill is a bottleneck single senior manager for complex claims that adds delay

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Total time in document collection phase 2002 from end of Evaluate to Approve 24h15m about 94 percent of total lead time 2003 from 09 40 to Apr 2 16 00 30h20m about 63 percent of total 2005 from 09 50 to Apr 4 10 00 72h10m about 94 percent of total This strongly shows the document request loop is the main bottleneck

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations Front load document requirements use checklists at submission for high complexity claims so all documents are requested once rather than iteratively Complexity triage route high complexity claims to specialized senior adjusters or a dedicated team Automate or parallelize allow customers to upload documents via portal automated reminders SLA timers on customer responses Balance manager workload consider adding capacity Review Region B document handling investigate why Lisa document rounds in Region B take longer Payment step for high complexity pre approval checks payment pre processing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Also worth noting with only 5 cases the sample is small so conclusions should be hypotheses to validate with more data
